headline: Constellation Brands Resets Long Term Outlook As New CEO Takes Over
summary: Constellation Brands (NYSE:STZ) withdrew its previously issued long term fiscal 2028 guidance. The company appointed Nicholas Fink as its new CEO, signaling a leadership transition at a challenging time for demand. These changes arrive as Constellation Brands contends with softer trends across key product categories. headline: Constellation Brands Sees Signs of Spending Rebound From Hispanic Consumers
summary: Shares of Constellation Brands, whose beer brands include Modelo, Corona and Pacifico, rose about 6% Thursday.  The company is seeing signs that Hispanic consumers are buying the company’s brews again.  “It’s too early to declare victory, but the trends have been more positive,” said Chief Executive Bill Newlands in an interview.

headline: Modelo Owner Constellation Gives Softer-Than-Expected Outlook
summary: Constellation Brands Inc. projected slower-than-expected growth for its beer business this year, renewing concern that declines in alcohol consumption have yet to hit their floor.  Organic beer sales, which measure changes in sales volumes, are seen in a range of down 1% to up 1% in the company's current year, Constellation said Wednesday. The range trails analysts' average estimate for a 2.2% increase.  While the guidance represents an improvement from the previous fiscal year, which ended Feb. 28, the lower-than-expected outlook suggests even popular brands such as Modelo and Corona are struggling to sidestep a broader decline in the category.

content.title: Modelo Owner Constellation Gives Softer-Than-Expected Outlook
content.description:
Constellation Brands Inc. projected slower-than-expected growth for its beer business this year, renewing concern that declines in alcohol consumption have yet to hit their floor. 
Organic beer sales, which measure changes in sales volumes, are seen in a range of down 1% to up 1% in the company's current year, Constellation said Wednesday. The range trails analysts' average estimate for a 2.2% increase. 
While the guidance represents an improvement from the previous fiscal year, which ended Feb. 28, the lower-than-expected outlook suggests even popular brands such as Modelo and Corona are struggling to sidestep a broader decline in the category.
content.summary: Constellation Brands Inc. projected slower-than-expected growth for its beer business this year, renewing concern that declines in alcohol consumption have yet to hit their floor.  Organic beer sales, which measure changes in sales volumes, are seen in a range of down 1% to up 1% in the company's current year, Constellation said Wednesday. The range trails analysts' average estimate for a 2.2% increase.  While the guidance represents an improvement from the previous fiscal year, which ended Feb. 28, the lower-than-expected outlook suggests even popular brands such as Modelo and Corona are struggling to sidestep a broader decline in the category.
